title: Corollary 'exact-slice' misapplies Lemma geom(3) at proper subgroups (degree mismatch)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
In the proof of Corollary cor:exact-slice ('$\Sigma^k HM$ is exactly a $(k r_G)$-slice'), the nulling of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}$ uses 'Lemma geom(3) applied over each $H$'. But geom(3) is proved only at the TOP group $G$ and gives $[N^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM]^G=0$ when $|T|>k r_G$.

To null a generator $G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1$ with H PROPER and $|T|>k r_G$, one needs (by adjunction) $[N^TS^1,\Sigma^k i_H^*HM]^H=0$, and the H-level geom(3) only gives this when $|T|>k r_H$ with $r_H=[H:\chi(H)]$. Since $r_H$ can be SMALLER than $r_G$, the hypothesis $|T|>k r_G$ does NOT imply $|T|>k r_H$, so the vanishing is unavailable for the relevant generators. The exact-slice claim is therefore not established; the correct statement likely involves the per-$H$ degrees rather than a single $r_G$, and $\Sigma^kHM$ may not even be an exact slice in the incomplete setting.

Note: this Corollary is NOT used in the proof of the Main Theorem (the converse uses only geom(2)), so it does not invalidate the theorem — but it is a genuine error in a stated-and-proved auxiliary result and should be corrected or deleted.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Re-review against the current `solution.tex`: this specific error is no longer present.

The current `cor:exact-slice` does **not** apply `lem:geom`(3) at proper $H$. For proper $H\subsetneq G$ it instead argues that $i_H^*HM\simeq 0$, hence $i_H^*\Sigma^k HM\simeq 0$, so $[N^TS^1, i_H^*\Sigma^kHM]^H = 0$ trivially. That step is correct: for a geometric Mackey functor $M=\underline A^{\mathrm{geo}}$, $(i_H^*M)(H/K)=M(G/K)=0$ for all $K\subseteq H$ (proper in $G$), so $i_H^*HM$ has vanishing homotopy Mackey functor and is contractible. Thus the degree-mismatch ($r_H$ vs $r_G$) the issue describes does not arise.

**Residual (minor):** the nullity is checked only on generators $G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1$ with $|T|>kr_G$ (and implicitly their non-negative suspensions); the propagation of nullity through (o1)--(o3) to all of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}$ should cite the closure mechanism already established in `lem:cover-conn`. I would downgrade this issue to that one-sentence residual. Note `cor:exact-slice` is not used by `thm:main`.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolved: closure mechanism now made explicit in `cor:exact-slice`

**Status of the original error.** The degree-mismatch error (applying `lem:geom`(3) at proper $H$ with $r_H<r_G$) is genuinely absent: at proper $H'\subsetneq G$ the proof uses $i_{H'}^\ast HM\simeq0$ (so $i_{H'}^\ast\Sigma^kHM\simeq0$ and the mapping group vanishes trivially), invoking **no** $H'$-level geometric bound. I have made this explicit and flagged it in-text.

**Residual gap addressed.** The previous proof only checked $[\,G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM]^G=0$ on the bare generators and asserted nullity of all of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}$ without justifying the propagation through (o1)–(o3). I rewrote the proof to supply this rigorously:

1. Restated the goal via the nullification universal property: $\Sigma^kHM\to P^{kr_G}_{\mathcal O}\Sigma^kHM$ is an equivalence $\iff$ $[X,\Sigma^kHM]^G=0$ for **all** $X\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}=\langle\mathcal G\rangle_{\ge}$.

2. Introduced the closure class $\mathcal N_W=\{X:[\Sigma^\ell(G/K_+\otimes X),W]^G=0\ \forall K\subseteq G,\ \ell\ge0\}$, with $W=\Sigma^kHM$. Crucially this is the $\pi_{\ge0}$-mapping-spectrum (not contractible-mapping-spectrum) version, and it is wrapped in $G/K_+$ so that it is genuinely closed under **(o3)** orbit tensors (via the double-coset splitting $G/K_+\otimes G/K'_+\simeq\bigsqcup G/(K\cap gK'g^{-1})_+$). I verified closure under (o1) (positive suspensions, cofibers/extensions, and homotopy colimits — including the $\lim^1$ argument for filtered colimits), (o2) retracts, and (o3).

   *(Remark: the contractible-mapping-spectrum class used in `lem:cover-conn` does **not** work here, because $\mathrm{Map}^G(C,\Sigma^kHM)$ has nonzero negative homotopy; the correct nullity notion only constrains $\pi_{\ge0}$, which is exactly what the suspension-closed subcategory sees.)*

3. Showed $\mathcal G\subseteq\mathcal N_W$: $G/K_+\otimes(G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1)$ is, by the projection formula and the norm–restriction formula `eq:resnorm`, a finite wedge of admissible induced cells $G_+\otimes_{L_j}N^{i_{L_j}^\ast T}S^1$ of the **same** underlying cardinality $|T|>kr_G$, hence again in $\mathcal G$. Vanishing on each such (suspended) generator is then the two-case check: $H'=G$ via `lem:geom`(3) applied to $T'\sqcup\ell\ast$ (cardinality $>kr_G$), and proper $H'$ via $i_{H'}^\ast\Sigma^kHM\simeq0$.

Therefore $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}\subseteq\mathcal N_W$, giving $[X,\Sigma^kHM]^G=0$ for all such $X$, i.e. $\Sigma^kHM$ is $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}$-null and exactly a $(kr_G)$-slice.

**Honest caveats.** The argument relies on standard inputs already used elsewhere in the solution: the projection/norm–restriction formula (`eq:resnorm`), `lem:geom`(3), `lem:index`(3), and $i_{H'}^\ast HM\simeq0$ for geometric $M$ at proper $H'$. The double-coset orbit-product splitting and the $\lim^1$ short exact sequence are standard. `cor:exact-slice` remains independent of `thm:main`. No LaTeX toolchain is available in the workspace, so I verified structural/brace balance by script rather than a full compile.
